opp.ais_why:
• Yurman procurement leads explicitly want to 'embed an AI-driven Teams assistant for conversational procurement tasks without system navigation' - a direct agentic-intake request.
• They also called out leveraging AI-enhanced OCR and GenAI for invoice capture targeting high-90s accuracy, showing GenAI is already in their requirements language.
• Brand-new indirect function under Kara Fisher means greenfield buy; one strong 32-min call plus active SF cadence puts AI fit at high confidence, even with IT-resource stall.

sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Kara Fisher
Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Julio Rivera
metrics.narrative: David Yurman articulated clear operational pain but provided no quantified financial impact or ROI target. Kara Fisher (Director of Indirect Procurement) stated on the 1 October 2025 discovery call that the newly established indirect procurement function (8 months old at the time) is fully manual and they are working from the ground up, with the goal of implementing a procure-to-pay system partnering with finance. Julio Rivera (AP Manager) explained that the current 2021 indirect procurement tool is clunky, non-intuitive, causing user adoption issues, and lacks critical functionality like delegation and out-of-office routing, forcing AP staff to spend time on IT troubleshooting rather than finance work. The buyer seeks centralized spend visibility across 50+ retail stores, process standardization, and cost savings driven by rapid company growth. However, no quantified savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, or ROI figure was stated. The next move is to re-engage and co-build a quantified business case with finance (CFO or finance leadership) tying the pain to measurable impact.

sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025: Kara Fisher coordinated meeting, operational-level engagement only
Next Step 5 January 2026: owner outreach to Kara, no reply, deal stalled per IT resource constraint
champion.narrative: No champion exists. Kara Fisher (Director of Indirect Procurement) was the primary contact and coordinated the 1 October 2025 discovery call, positioning her as a potential developing champion, but she has provided no evidence of advocacy, no internal introductions, no push on our behalf, and no engagement for 149 days (last activity 28 January 2026). The Next Step from 5 January 2026 shows the owner sent Kara a New Year message and connect request for the 2026 initiative, but received no reply; the note explicitly states the deal stalled due to IT resources committed to other projects. There is no coach, no multi-threading beyond the initial operational trio (Kara, Julio, Karilyn), and no access to the Economic Buyer (CFO Christopher Clipper) or any Decision Maker. Kara's silence and the stated IT resource constraint suggest she either cannot or will not advocate internally to secure prioritization. The current state is single-threaded, no champion, and relationship at risk of going permanently cold. The highest-priority move is to re-engage Kara with a value trigger (peer case study, ROI model, or executive-level outreach) to test whether she can be reactivated as a champion, or pivot to find a new internal sponsor.

sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Julio Rivera: stated talking to multiple vendors, did not name any
competition.narrative: Competition is entirely unmapped. No competitor was named by the buyer on the 1 October 2025 discovery call, and the Salesforce Competitors field is empty. Julio Rivera stated they are talking to multiple vendors and are evaluating fit, but did not name any other vendor being considered. The discovery call ended with Julio saying they would check back internally and provide feedback on whether Zycus is a good match, indicating an active evaluation, but no shortlist, RFP process, or named alternatives have been disclosed. The deal has been dark for 149 days with no follow-on engagement, so the competitive landscape may have evolved or the buyer may have selected another vendor and not informed Zycus. The next move is to re-engage and directly ask who else is being evaluated, where Zycus stands in the shortlist, and what the decision timeline is.

sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Kara Fisher: manual processes, no P2P system, 50+ store free-for-all, rapid growth
Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Julio Rivera: clunky tool, missing standard features, AP doing IT troubleshooting
identify_pain.narrative: David Yurman articulated clear, multi-faceted operational pain on the 1 October 2025 discovery call. The pain owners are Kara Fisher (Director of Indirect Procurement) and Julio Rivera (AP Manager). Kara stated the indirect procurement function is brand new (8 months old at time of call), built from the ground up, and currently fully manual with no procure-to-pay system in place. She described rapid company growth driving the need to centralize spend visibility and enforce uniform purchasing across 50+ retail stores, which today is a free-for-all. Julio described the 2021 indirect procurement tool as clunky, non-intuitive, and missing expected-to-be-standard features like delegation and out-of-office routing, causing frequent system issues and forcing AP staff to spend time on IT troubleshooting instead of finance work. He stated the tool lacks real-time workflow connected to their ERP, vendor portal, parallel approvals, and budget controls. The pain is operational inefficiency, lack of spend visibility, user adoption failure, and AP/procurement staff diverted from value-add work to system troubleshooting. The pain is confirmed and owned; the gap is that no quantified financial impact (cost, time, error rate, savings opportunity) has been stated, and the CFO or senior finance leader has not validated the pain as a funded priority.

sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025: no contracting or legal discussion, NDA not yet signed
Next Step 5 January 2026: IT resource constraint mentioned but process unclear
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec, and partner involvement are entirely unmapped. The 1 October 2025 discovery call did not advance to commercial or contracting discussion; the buyer declined to share spend scope citing the absence of an NDA. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal review process, InfoSec questionnaire, or approval timeline has been discussed. No systems integrator or implementation partner has been mentioned. The Next Step from 5 January 2026 indicates IT resource constraints are blocking the initiative, but whether IT is a formal gate in the contracting or implementation process is unknown. The deal is still in early Qualified stage with no pricing, proposal, or contract engagement. Visibility will be created only when the buyer re-engages and advances to commercial discussions, at which point legal, InfoSec, and contracting process must be mapped.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: Christopher Clipper, CFO, Executive Sponsor
Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025: operational-only attendees, no CFO present
economic_buyer.narrative: Christopher Clipper (CFO) is listed as Executive Sponsor in the Salesforce opportunity contact roles, indicating senior financial authority, but he has never appeared on a call or engaged directly in this sales cycle. The single discovery call on 1 October 2025 was with operational stakeholders only: Kara Fisher (Director of Indirect Procurement), Julio Rivera (AP Manager), and Karilyn (AP Supervisor). Julio stated the initiative is a partnership between procurement and finance to implement a procure-to-pay system, and referenced budget and spend scope but declined to share specifics citing the absence of an NDA. No commercial, pricing, or contract conversation has occurred. The CFO's passive Executive Sponsor status without active engagement is a significant gap; economic buyer authority and budget approval process are unmapped. The next move is to secure a direct meeting with the CFO or senior finance leadership to validate budget, authority, and commercial decision process before advancing to pricing or proposal.

sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025: operational-only attendees, internal review mentioned by Julio Rivera
Next Step 5 January 2026: stalled due to IT resources committed to other projects
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: CFO as Executive Sponsor, no engagement
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially visible but incomplete. The 1 October 2025 discovery call sequence shows operational-level evaluation: Kara Fisher (Indirect Procurement Director) coordinating the meeting, Julio Rivera (AP Manager) and Karilyn (AP Supervisor) articulating requirements. Julio stated at the end of the call that they would check back internally with Kara and the team to evaluate the Zycus solution and provide feedback on vendor fit, indicating an internal review process. The Next Step note from 5 January 2026 states the deal stalled due to IT resources committed to other projects, revealing that IT capacity and prioritization is a gate, but the approval chain, committee structure, and executive decision authority are unmapped. The CFO (Christopher Clipper) is listed as Executive Sponsor but has never engaged. No Decision Maker contact role exists. The buyer referenced talking to multiple vendors but did not describe the shortlisting, scoring, or final selection process. The gap: who makes the final vendor decision, what is the approval chain, when is the decision timeline, and how does IT resource availability fit into the process.

sources: Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Julio Rivera: maturity, ease-of-use, delegation, parallel approvals, ERP integration, vendor portal
Avoma discovery call 1 October 2025, Kara Fisher: full-cycle P2P, internal catalog, 50+ store centralization
decision_criteria.narrative: David Yurman articulated detailed functional evaluation criteria on the 1 October 2025 discovery call. Julio Rivera stated they are evaluating multiple vendors and seeking a system that is intuitive and easy to use to minimize IT support overhead, with mature standard functionality including delegation, out-of-office routing, real-time workflow connected to their ERP, vendor portal and onboarding, parallel approvals, budget controls, and hierarchical business rules. He emphasized past pain with the 2021 tool where expected-to-be-standard features were missing or broken, causing AP staff to become IT troubleshooters. Kara Fisher added the need for a full-cycle procure-to-pay solution spanning RFP, indirect procurement, requisitioning, PO generation, invoice matching, and voucher approval, with an internal Amazon-like catalog for stores to order from approved suppliers at negotiated pricing. The payment execution will remain in their bank-integrated ERP, so the P2P tool must feed approved vouchers back to the ERP. These are clear, detailed, use-case-driven criteria; the gap is that no formal RFP document, BRD, or weighted scorecard has been shared, and no validation or demo against these criteria has occurred since the discovery call 9 months ago.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS fields are null (no Salesforce AI excitement signal). The buyer's articulated pain (clunky tool, IT troubleshooting overhead, need for intuitive self-service) maps well to Merlin's conversational AI assistant, and the product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake. Julio Rivera responded positively to the AI pitch on the discovery call ('really interesting food for thought'), but no quantified AI use case, ROI, or adoption metric was discussed. The buyer did not explicitly state an AI mandate or appetite, but their operational pain (ease of use, minimal IT support) is a natural AI-curious fit. After 149 days of silence, AI positioning must be refreshed with a tangible retail peer proof point.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal has gone cold after a well-received discovery call. Positive initial engagement (buyer articulated pain, stated Zycus checks the boxes, committed to internal review) followed by 149 days of silence. The stated IT resource constraint and lack of response to owner outreach signal deprioritization or a polite decline. No champion, no economic buyer engagement, single-threaded relationship at high risk. Confidence signals are weak; deal requires urgent re-engagement or disqualification.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2 October 2025, currently in Qualified stage (264 days in stage as of 26 June 2026). Last verified buyer activity was 28 January 2026 (149 days ago). Owner reached out 5 January 2026 with a New Year message and connect request, but received no response; Next Step explicitly states deal stalled due to IT resources committed to other projects. No forward movement since. Close date 28 February 2027 is 247 days out, so no near-term urgency, but 149 days of silence in an early Qualified stage with no champion, no multi-threading, and a stated resource constraint blocking progress means the deal is cold and off track. Forecast category Pipeline is appropriate given stage and engagement state, but the deal requires full re-engagement to restart momentum.
evidence: Next Step dated 5 January 2026: owner sent New Year message and connect request, no buyer reply; explicitly states stalled due to IT resources committed to other projects
Single discovery call 1 October 2025 (~9 months ago), no follow-on buyer engagement

Stage Qualified for 264 days with no progression
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains completely dark for 149 days since last buyer contact on 28 January 2026. Owner's January outreach went unanswered; IT resource constraints stalled the initiative. No champion, single-threaded, and cold in early Qualified stage with no visible path forward.

flags: Secure economic buyer sponsorship before advancing: the CFO is listed as Executive Sponsor but has never engaged. Without CFO validation of the business case, budget, and priority, this deal cannot move to proposal or pricing. The next milestone for a Qualified-stage deal is to map the economic buyer, build a quantified ROI case with finance, and secure active sponsorship. Until the CFO or a finance VP confirms this is a funded 2026-2027 priority, the deal is at risk of indefinite stall.
best_practice_check.summary: This deal is dark, single-threaded, and stalled by a stated IT resource constraint with no champion, no economic buyer engagement, and no mapped decision authority. The highest-leverage actions are to re-engage with a value trigger to restart the relationship, immediately multi-thread to finance and IT leadership to bypass the stated blocker, and secure executive sponsorship (CFO or finance VP) to validate budget and prioritization.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is entirely unknown. No competitor was named on the 1 October 2025 discovery call or in Salesforce. Julio Rivera stated they are evaluating multiple vendors but did not disclose names or the shortlist. After 149 days of silence, Zycus may have been eliminated or deprioritized without notification. Re-engagement must immediately surface the competitive landscape, Zycus's standing, and the buyer's decision timeline.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/Merlin was positioned on the discovery call as a key differentiator (Teams-embedded conversational assistant, PwC ease-of-use case study, Gen AI for invoice capture). Julio Rivera's top pain point was a clunky, non-intuitive tool causing IT troubleshooting overhead, and the Merlin Teams assistant directly solves that (no training, conversational interface, self-service). The AI story resonated as 'interesting food for thought,' but no validation, demo, or deeper AI use-case discussion has occurred since. AIS fields are null (no Salesforce AI excitement score), and the product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake, indicating AI is in scope. The gap is that AI fit has not been tested or confirmed beyond the pitch, and after 149 days of silence the buyer may not recall or value it. The next move is to re-anchor the AI narrative with a retail peer case study (e.g., how a jewelry or luxury retail client used Merlin to eliminate AP manual work and achieve rapid adoption) as part of the re-engagement.

items.quote: We're looking for a system that is very easy to use. We've encountered issues with people using the system that we currently have, clunky, not being as intuitive. We're looking to become more AP finance personnel than IT troubleshoot personnel.
items.criterion: Intuitive, easy-to-use system to minimize IT support overhead

items.quote: A lot of the issues we've encountered with the current system were things we expected to be standard. Delegations has been a problem. Out of office whenever somebody goes out of office, it's been a problem. We are looking for parallel approvals so that if something needs to be approved by two different people, that can go concurrently.
items.criterion: Mature standard functionality: delegation, out-of-office routing, parallel approvals

items.quote: We are looking for a system that can do the workflow almost on a real time basis with a connection to our ERP system, that can do a vendor portal and a vendor onboarding.
items.criterion: Real-time workflow connected to ERP, vendor portal and onboarding

items.quote: We are looking to do something that's much more holistic, a full cycle situation which goes through the RFP process, indirect procurement, into a requisition, procure to pay. The payment still needs to go to the ERP at the end.
items.criterion: Full-cycle procure-to-pay: RFP, requisitioning, PO, invoice matching, voucher approval

items.quote: We want a place where we can catalog and make purchases for all of our stores, like an Amazon shop or a one stop shop where we could centralize the buying and purchasing power internally.
items.criterion: Internal Amazon-like catalog for 50+ retail stores to order from approved suppliers at negotiated pricing

items.quote: Allowing your teams the ability to do everything you want to do in the procurement and AP automation function right through Teams, conversational interaction with an agent so they don't even have to understand the prompts. That's the kind of ease of use we're offering today. I think this is one of the fundamental reasons that PwC chose us.
items.criterion: AI-driven Teams assistant for conversational procurement without system navigation

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional alignment on ease of use, mature P2P workflow, ERP integration, vendor portal, parallel approvals, delegation, and catalog-driven requisitioning. Zycus's Teams-embedded Merlin assistant directly addresses the buyer's top pain point (clunky, non-intuitive tool causing IT troubleshooting overhead). Spend analytics, sourcing, CLM, and eInvoicing AI capabilities match the full-cycle P2P and centralized visibility goals. The fit case was presented on the discovery call and resonated (Julio: 'definitely has a lot of the boxes we were thinking, really interesting food for thought'). No validation demo, POC, or deeper fit session has occurred since. The gap is that fit has not been tested or confirmed beyond the initial discovery pitch.

hard.ais_why:
• Yurman procurement leads explicitly want to 'embed an AI-driven Teams assistant for conversational procurement tasks without system navigation' - a direct agentic-intake request.
• They also called out leveraging AI-enhanced OCR and GenAI for invoice capture targeting high-90s accuracy, showing GenAI is already in their requirements language.
• Brand-new indirect function under Kara Fisher means greenfield buy; one strong 32-min call plus active SF cadence puts AI fit at high confidence, even with IT-resource stall.

deltas.subject: Multi-thread to finance and IT leadership urgently: single-threaded to an indirect procurement director who has gone silent is a ghost risk. The CFO (Christopher Clipper, Executive Sponsor) and the ERP/IT manager must be engaged directly to validate budget authority, IT capacity planning, and decision timeline. The stated IT resource constraint is a gate; the next move is to surface whether IT involvement is a near-term or long-term blocker, and position Zycus's low-IT-overhead, self-service approach (Teams assistant, minimal integration lift) as the solution that frees IT rather than consuming it.

deltas.to: Secure economic buyer sponsorship before advancing: the CFO is listed as Executive Sponsor but has never engaged. Without CFO validation of the business case, b
deltas.subject: Secure economic buyer sponsorship before advancing: the CFO is listed as Executive Sponsor but has never engaged. Without CFO validation of the business case, budget, and priority, this deal cannot move to proposal or pricing. The next milestone for a Qualified-stage deal is to map the economic buyer, build a quantified ROI case with finance, and secure active sponsorship. Until the CFO or a finance VP confirms this is a funded 2026-2027 priority, the deal is at risk of indefinite stall.

value.flag: Re-engage the dormant relationship immediately: 149 days of silence after a well-received discovery call signals either deprioritization or elimination. The stated IT resource constraint may be a polite decline or a real blocker; test Kara Fisher's responsiveness with a high-value, low-effort trigger (peer retail case study showing rapid ROI, or an executive-level outreach from the deal owner's manager to the CFO) within the next 7 days. If no reply, pivot to find a new internal sponsor or disqualify.
